monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Pan-Philippine maritime folklore with Spanish-era and indigenous sea-spirit influences
monsters.passive_ability: Song of the Tides — Healing, charm, and sound skills become stronger near water, during Moonlit weather, or in Sea Breeze conditions.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Harana ng Alon — Sings a wave-charged melody that heals allies and may Calm Fear, Despair, or Frenzy.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Drowning Lullaby — Sends a haunting song across the battlefield, applying Sleep or Slow to enemies and dealing Sea/Sound damage.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heal, Water Wave, Sea Song, Moon Veil, Calm, Cleanse, Haste, Barrier, Soaked, Spirit Ward, Reveal, Regeneration, Blessing, Tide Step, Sound Pulse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires returning a stolen shell instrument and saving a drowning sailor without demanding reward
monsters.habitat: Coral Reef, Open Sea, Fishing Village, Hidden Lake, Waterfall Sanctuary
monsters.weather_preference: Moonlit, Sea Breeze, Rain, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Shell comb, pearl rice cake, sea grapes, moonlit water, woven song ribbon
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Sirena are among the most familiar sea Nilalang in the archipelago: beautiful, dangerous, sorrowful, and often misunderstood. Many humans describe them only as singers who lure sailors to the waves, but ALAMAT treats their songs as language, warning, memory, and mourning. This Sirena once guided fishing boats away from reefs, typhoons, and sea-monster routes. Her voice became feared after a band of smugglers stole her shell instrument and used a false imitation of her song to wreck rival boats. The player must recover the shell, rescue a sailor trapped by guilt, and let the village hear the true song again. When bonded, the Sirena becomes a support Nilalang who heals through melody, manipulates turn flow with lullabies, and protects the party from fear. Her trust story teaches that beauty can be weaponized by liars, but restored by truth.
monsters.folklore_summary: Sirena in Philippine folklore are mermaid-like sea beings, often described as beautiful women with fish tails who sing near the sea. They are connected with enchantment, drowning stories, maritime mystery, and coastal superstition.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Sirena as tide-song spirits whose voices carry warnings, grief, and healing. Some lure humans to death, but others guard coastal communities from the dangers of the sea.
monsters.physical_description: A graceful mermaid-like spirit with warm brown skin, long black hair decorated with shells and sea flowers, shimmering blue-green tail scales, translucent fin veils, pearl ornaments, and a glowing shell instrument held near her chest.
monsters.weaknesses: Drought, Silence, Poisoned water, broken shell instruments, lightning disruption
monsters.resistances: Sea, Sound, Sleep, Charm, Fear, Soaked
monsters.npc_related: Lira the shell singer; Ka Ambo the old fisherman; Naya the rescued sailor
monsters.boss_variant: Sirena ng Malalim na Awit — controls turn order through layered songs and tidal barriers.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Sirena ng Basag na Tinig — sings cracked melodies that cause Despair and Sleep.
monsters.divine_variant: Himig ng Malinis na Dagat — radiant sea singer whose song cleanses poisoned waters.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A gentle melodic vocalization layered with surf, shell chimes, and a lonely descending note.
monsters.drop_items: Shell Song Fragment, Sirena Scale, Tidal Thread, Moonlit Pearl Dust
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Shell Charm, Instrument, Beadwork, Woven Garb, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Sirena inspired by Philippine folklore: graceful mermaid sea spirit, warm brown skin, long black hair with shells, blue-green tail scales, pearl ornaments, glowing shell instrument, moonlit water aura, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG support character style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Filipino Sirena mermaid, long black hair, shell ornaments, shimmering blue-green tail, glowing shell instrument, moonlit sea, compassionate but mysterious expression, painterly RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Sirena raises a glowing shell instrument, concentric blue sound waves ripple across the battlefield, allies are wrapped in healing tide-light, then enemies are slowed by a haunting lullaby carried on foam.
monsters.design_notes: Differentiate Sirena from Kataw: Sirena is music-, charm-, and emotional-healing focused, while Kataw is oath-, mirror-, and tide-law focused.

monsters.province_origin: Albay, Camarines Sur, Sorsogon, and Bicol coastal traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Reef Ambusher — Gains critical chance against Soaked, Slowed, or Marked enemies. Stronger in Coral Reef and Open Sea habitats.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Red-Tide Spear Tail — Strikes with a bladed tail, dealing Sea/Blood damage and applying Bleed.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Riptide Huntress — Dashes through water currents, becoming Hidden, then attacks the lowest-HP enemy with bonus critical damage.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Water Strike, Quick Slash, Blood Drain, Ambush, Mark Prey, Haste, Evasive Step, Sea Roar, Soaked, Bleed Mark, Counter Ready, Tide Step, Guard Break, Fear Cry, Dark Pulse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires defending a reef nursery and proving restraint in battle
monsters.habitat: Coral Reef, Open Sea, Storm Coast, Mangrove Marsh
monsters.weather_preference: Rain, Heavy Rain, Typhoon, Sea Breeze
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Red sea flowers, polished spear shell, clean fish, coral-safe charm, warrior oath
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Magindara are remembered in Bicolano traditions as beautiful but dangerous sea beings, sometimes described as mermaid-like creatures with fierce temperaments and deadly power. In ALAMAT, Magindara are warrior guardians of reef nurseries, storm passages, and spawning grounds. Unlike Sirena, who fights through song, or Kataw, who mediates oaths, Magindara protects through speed, teeth, spear-tail strikes, and frightening precision. This Magindara is first encountered after fishing boats vanish near a reef. The village believes she is attacking without reason, but the player discovers that poachers have been harvesting eggs from a sacred reef nursery. To earn trust, the player must stop the poachers, return the reef stones, and defeat the Magindara in an honorable duel without killing any sea creature. Once bonded, she becomes a fast aquatic assassin built around ambush, bleed, and marked prey.
monsters.folklore_summary: Magindara are Bicolano sea beings often depicted as beautiful but dangerous mermaid-like creatures. They are associated with the sea, enchantment, and sometimes hostility toward humans.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Magindara as fierce reef guardians and warrior spirits, dangerous because they protect vulnerable sea life from human exploitation.
monsters.physical_description: A fierce merfolk warrior with dark sea-green scales, sharp fin crests, glowing teal eyes, clawed webbed hands, a spear-like tail fin edged with red coral, shell-and-bone armor, and battle scars across her shoulders.
monsters.weaknesses: Lightning, Drought, calm rituals, poisoned reef water, broken dueling oaths
monsters.resistances: Sea, Blood, Bleed, Fear, Soaked, speed reduction in water
monsters.npc_related: Datu Baybay; Naya the freed diver; Lira the Sirena; Apo Marikit of the reef shrine
monsters.boss_variant: Magindara Punong-Mandirigma — uses chain ambushes and reef hazards.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Duguang Magindara — loses restraint and bleeds enemies with every water strike.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Bahurang Pula — coral-armored guardian who protects reef nurseries.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A sharp war-cry that cuts through surf, followed by a hiss of rushing tide.
monsters.drop_items: Red Coral Blade, Magindara Scale, Reef Blood Pearl, Spear-Tail Fin
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Shell Charm, Relic Weapon, Beadwork, Tattoo Mark, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Magindara inspired by Bicolano sea folklore: fierce merfolk warrior, dark sea-green scales, sharp fin crests, glowing teal eyes, red coral spear-tail, shell-and-bone armor, dynamic ambush pose, transparent background, readable chibi RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Magindara sea guardian from Bicolano folklore, fierce mermaid warrior, red coral spear-tail visible, teal glowing eyes, shell armor, stormy reef background, proud and dangerous RPG creature art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Magindara vanishes into a red tide current, a spear-shaped tail flashes across the screen, the target is marked by coral-red water, and she reappears behind them with a reef-splash burst.
monsters.design_notes: Make Magindara more martial and predatory than Sirena or Kataw. Strong assassin identity.

monsters.province_origin: Bagobo, Manobo, and other Mindanao highland traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Carrion Frenzy — Gains attack when any unit is defeated. If corrupted, also gains speed but loses spirit defense.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Grave-Maw Rend — Tears into one enemy with Underworld/Beast damage, dealing bonus damage to weakened or cursed targets.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Carrion Moon Howl — Howls over the battlefield, applying Fear and Weakened to all enemies while granting Frenzy to itself.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Bite, Heavy Strike, Fear Cry, Curse Bite, Reckless Charge, Blood Drain, Rot, Weaken, Dark Pulse, Guard Break, Stomp, Life Siphon, Frenzy, Counter Ready, Poison Fang
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires cleansing corpse-ground corruption and honoring burial taboos
monsters.habitat: Graveyard, Corrupted Wilds, Limestone Cave, Spirit Threshold, Mountain Peak
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Burial flowers, ash bowl, bitter root, clean grave soil, name-stone
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Busaw are feared in several Mindanao traditions as corpse-eating beings or dangerous spirits associated with death and predation. In ALAMAT, Busaw are born where death is exploited, mocked, or left without ritual closure. They are drawn to battlefields, abandoned graves, and corrupted places where the boundary between the living and dead has been torn open. This Busaw is found near a mountain burial ground where miners disturbed ancestral remains while searching for black ore. The creature attacks anyone nearby, not because it understands justice, but because corruption has turned grief into hunger. To earn trust, the player must recover the bones, return them to proper rest, and withstand the Busaw's hunger without feeding it more death. Once purified enough to bond, Busaw becomes a high-risk berserker whose strength rises when the battle grows desperate.
monsters.folklore_summary: Busaw is described in various Mindanao traditions as a dangerous being or spirit, often associated with corpses, death, and predatory hunger. Details vary by ethnolinguistic community.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT interprets Busaw as a death-ground spirit twisted by broken burial respect and corruption, capable of being redirected into a guardian of taboo boundaries.
monsters.physical_description: A hunched muscular wild spirit with ash-black skin, bone-white markings, long claws, matted hair, glowing red-orange eyes, tusk-like teeth, and a ragged hide cloak. Its body emits black grave mist and ash flakes.
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred burial rites, Sun purification, bell wards, calm effects, ancestor-name rituals
monsters.resistances: Underworld, Fear, Rot, Poison, physical damage, Frenzy
monsters.npc_related: Apo Sandawa the burial elder; Miko the young miner; Bae Luntaya the bone keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Busaw ng Libingang Bato — grows stronger every time a grave marker is broken.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Salot-Busaw — spreads Rot and gains speed after each defeated unit.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Huling Pahinga — solemn burial guardian with ash-white aura.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A guttural howl like an animal and a mourning chant fused together.
monsters.drop_items: Grave Claw, Ash Hide, Bone Marking Shard, Black Ore Dust
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bone Talisman, Relic Weapon, Tattoo Mark, Totem, Bell
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Busaw inspired by Mindanao folklore: hunched corpse-eating wild spirit, ash-black skin, bone-white markings, glowing red-orange eyes, long claws, ragged hide cloak, grave mist aura, transparent background, readable chibi RPG berserker silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Busaw, a Mindanao folklore corpse-eating spirit, ash-black muscular body, bone markings, matted hair, red-orange eyes, grave mist, mountain burial ground atmosphere, fierce and tragic RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Busaw crouches low, grave ash spirals around it, it howls under a dark moon, then lunges with both claws as bone-white markings flare across its body.
monsters.design_notes: Handle death and burial themes respectfully. Emphasize taboo, grief, and corruption rather than gore.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Bagobo folklore and related Mindanao oral traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Rot-Hide Bulk — Takes reduced damage while affected by Rot or Poison; healing received is reduced unless purified.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Rotting Forest Grasp — Grabs one enemy with corrupted roots and claws, dealing Corruption/Nature damage and applying Rot.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Ghoul's Hunger Wall — Raises a wall of corrupted forest growth, shielding itself and taunting enemies for two turns.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Taunt, Rot, Poison, Root Prison, Heavy Strike, Fortify, Weaken, Fear Cry, Stone Skin, Nature Shield, Counter Ready, Protect Ally, Dark Pulse, Slow
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires separating the ghoul from corrupted forest hunger
monsters.habitat: Corrupted Wilds, Balete Grove, Limestone Cave, Mountain Peak, Graveyard
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Bitter roots, clean soil, ash bowl, forest fruit, purification herb bundle
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Buso are described in Mindanao traditions as frightening ghoul-like beings, often connected with forests, death, and predatory hunger. In ALAMAT, Buso represent what happens when a forest's guardian cycle breaks and the land begins feeding on fear instead of life. This Buso was once a territorial forest being that consumed decay and returned it to the soil. After corruption spread through a logging camp and burial site, it forgot the difference between dead matter and living travelers. The player must restore the forest's cycle: bury the dead properly, burn diseased roots in a controlled ritual, and plant new growth where the Buso first appeared. Once bonded, Buso becomes a slow but durable tank that uses rot, taunt, and corrupted-root defenses. It is ugly, frightening, and heavy, but not beyond redemption.
monsters.folklore_summary: Buso is known in Bagobo and other Mindanao folklore as a ghoul-like being, often depicted as frightening, corpse-eating, or dangerous. Specific descriptions vary across oral traditions and retellings.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Buso as a corrupted decay-cycle guardian: a being meant to consume rot that became trapped in hunger and imbalance.
monsters.physical_description: A large, heavy forest ghoul with green-gray skin, moss patches, fungal growths, root-like veins, tusked mouth, thick arms, hunched posture, and a belly marked by glowing corruption cracks. It drags roots behind like chains.
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred purification, Fire cleansing, Sun growth rites, anti-rot herbs, restored forest terrain
monsters.resistances: Corruption, Forest, Poison, Rot, Fear, physical impact, taunt effects
monsters.npc_related: Bae Luntaya the herbalist; Taga-Bukid Ramo; Miko the lost logger
monsters.boss_variant: Buso ng Nabubulok na Gubat — creates rot zones that heal it and weaken enemies.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Ganid na Buso — fully consumed by corruption, spreads Rot every turn.
monsters.divine_variant: Tagapagbalik sa Lupa — cleansed decay guardian that turns poison into healing soil.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A wet forest groan mixed with dragging roots and a low hungry growl.
monsters.drop_items: Rot Root, Fungal Hide, Corruption Moss, Ghoul Tusk
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Herbal Bundle, Bone Talisman, Relic Weapon, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Buso inspired by Mindanao folklore: large forest ghoul tank, green-gray skin, moss and fungus growths, tusked mouth, thick arms, root chains, glowing corruption cracks, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Buso, Mindanao forest ghoul, mossy green-gray skin, fungal growths, tusks, root-like veins, hunched body, corrupted forest background, frightening but tragic RPG creature art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Buso slams both hands into the soil, black-green roots erupt around it as a defensive wall, rot spores burst outward, and enemies are dragged into taunt range.
monsters.design_notes: Differentiate Buso from Busaw: Buso is slow forest-rot tank; Busaw is aggressive burial-ground berserker.

monsters.passive_ability: Sky-Eclipse Wings — During Eclipse, Storm, or Moonlit weather, gains speed and evasion. Attacks against airborne or marked enemies gain bonus critical chance.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Star-Beak Descent — Dives from the upper sky, dealing Sky/Moon damage to one enemy and applying Marked.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Feast of the Eastern Moon — Spreads vast eclipse wings, damaging all enemies, reducing accuracy, and empowering allied Sky and Moon skills.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Wind Slash, Thunder Strike, Moon Veil, Haste, Mark Prey, Evasive Flight, Storm Call, Lunar Beam, Feather Guard, Reveal, Spirit Ward, Quick Slash, Sacred Light, Fear Cry, Counter Ready
monsters.capture_difficulty: Legendary Trial / Trust Difficulty: Requires completing the Eastern Eclipse sky trial
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Spirit Threshold, Storm Coast, Moonlit Clearing, Abandoned Watchtower
monsters.weather_preference: Eclipse, Moonlit, Thunderstorm, Typhoon, Spirit Glow
monsters.active_time: Eclipse, Full Moon, Dawn, Story Triggered
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Starfruit, seven black feathers, wind bell, moonlit water, sky-oath ribbon
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Minokawa is a gigantic celestial bird from Bagobo mythology, often connected with eclipse stories and the devouring of the moon or sun. In ALAMAT, Minokawa is the sky counterpart to Bakunawa's sea-cosmic mystery. Where Bakunawa coils through the sky-sea, Minokawa hunts across the upper winds, watching for cracks in the celestial order. People fear it because its wings can cover the moon, but older sky-watchers say it appears only when the heavens have already been wounded. The player first sees Minokawa as a silhouette crossing the eastern moon while storm winds tear through watchtowers. To earn its trust, the player must climb the Eastern Eclipse Trial, repair wind bells on three peaks, and refuse the temptation to shoot the bird down for glory. Once bonded, Minokawa is a legendary aerial ranger with extreme speed, mark-based attacks, and eclipse-wide battlefield control.
monsters.folklore_summary: Minokawa is a giant bird in Bagobo mythology, associated with eclipses and cosmic scale. It is sometimes described as large enough to swallow celestial bodies such as the moon or sun.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT portrays Minokawa as a celestial guardian-hunter whose eclipse wings signal danger in the sky rather than simple malice.
monsters.physical_description: A colossal bird of prey with black-blue feathers, silver moon-edge markings, golden eyes, a beak like polished obsidian, talons glowing with starlight, and wings so large they resemble night clouds filled with constellations.
monsters.weaknesses: Grounding Earth seals, broken wind-bell rituals, Sun purification, overconfidence bait, corrupted star-metal
monsters.resistances: Sky, Moon, Divine, Storm, Fear, Blind, forced movement, aerial attacks
monsters.npc_related: Apo Tala the eclipse astronomer; Bae Luntaya; Ka Ramil the wind-bell maker; Datu Baybay
monsters.boss_variant: Minokawa, Shadow Over the Eastern Moon — multi-phase aerial world boss with eclipse wind hazards.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Minokawa ng Basag na Bituin — celestial hunter consumed by broken star-metal.
monsters.divine_variant: Minokawa, Bantay ng Silangang Langit — radiant sky guardian whose wings restore moonlight.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A piercing eagle-like cry stretched into thunder, followed by wind bells ringing in the clouds.
monsters.drop_items: Eclipse Feather, Star Talon, Moon-Edge Plume, Wind-Bell Fragment
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bell, Relic Weapon, Feather Charm, Anting-Anting, Divine Talisman
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG legendary boss sprite of Minokawa inspired by Bagobo Philippine mythology: colossal celestial bird, black-blue feathers, silver moon-edge markings, golden eyes, obsidian beak, starlight talons, constellation wings, eclipse aura, transparent background, readable legendary RPG silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Epic fantasy portrait of Minokawa, giant eclipse bird from Bagobo mythology, black-blue feathers, silver moon markings, golden eyes, enormous wings covering moonlit sky, storm clouds and constellations, majestic legendary RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Minokawa rises above the battlefield, spreads constellation-filled wings until the moon darkens, wind bells ring violently, then it dives as a star-beak comet leaving a silver eclipse trail.
monsters.design_notes: Treat as a flagship Legendary paired thematically with Bakunawa. Minokawa should feel aerial, fast, proud, and cosmic.
